title: Visionary trace entry
description: Single event emitted by a hook or script into .visionary/traces/<session_id>.jsonl. One JSON object per line. The `event` enum covers every moment in a generation worth replaying for post-mortem debugging, recurring-fix mining, and future GEPA-style critic evolution. `payload` is free-form so each event type can attach its own facts without a schema bump — validators treat unknown payload keys as opaque.

session_id.description: ULID or similar time-sortable id that groups all events from one Claude Code session.

generation_id.description: ULID scoped to one component generation (typically one edited file). Multiple rounds share the same generation_id.

round.description: Round number within the generation. 0 is reserved for pre-round events like style_selected or brief_embedded. 1..3 are the critique rounds. Values above 3 are tolerated for future diff-refine loops.

ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp at emission. Used for latency analysis and retention-based rotation.

event.description: What happened. The enum is closed — unknown events should be added here before emission so stats tooling can reason about them.

payload.description: Event-specific facts. Free-form; documented shape per event lives in docs/trace-schema.md. Kept opaque here so adding a new field to an event's payload never requires a schema migration.

emitter.description: Name of the script or hook that emitted the event. Useful when multiple emitters can produce the same event type (e.g. critic_output from critic-craft and critic-aesthetic both have their own emitter tag).

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration of the operation that produced this event, when available. Consumed by `visionary-stats --latency`.
